purpose: Definition- and theorem-granularity Willard anchor map for the first AS formal-confidence claims. This map names the source loci AS must preserve before claiming any self-consistency, proof-code, or object-language result.

anchors.title: Self-Verifying Axiom Systems, the Incompleteness Theorem and Related Reflection Principles
anchors.summary: Defines Prenex* sentences and the bounded variants Phi_i_j used by later tangibility and reflection clauses.
anchors.next_as_action: When AS adds an SJAS arithmetic language layer, encode Prenex* and bounded-quantifier notation as explicit syntax classes rather than comments.

anchors.title: Self-Verifying Axiom Systems, the Incompleteness Theorem and Related Reflection Principles
anchors.summary: Defines tangibility predicates and the reflection principle that restricts self-reflection to sufficiently tangible numbers.
anchors.next_as_action: Map any AS self-confidence claim to a specific tangibility predicate or explicitly reject tangibility as out of scope.

anchors.title: Self-Verifying Axiom Systems, the Incompleteness Theorem and Related Reflection Principles
anchors.summary: Defines the IS(A) mapping through grouped axioms: constants, grounding functions, imported Pi-1 theorems, and a self-referential semantic-tableaux consistency axiom.
anchors.next_as_action: Treat IS(A)'s four groups as the first candidate schema for an AS formal-confidence layer over transition claims.

anchors.title: Self-Verifying Axiom Systems, the Incompleteness Theorem and Related Reflection Principles
anchors.summary: States that regular consistency of A implies consistency of IS(A), making IS(.) a consistency-preserving mapping under the paper's assumptions.
anchors.next_as_action: Before AS claims any consistency-preserving map, record its exact base-consistency assumption and proof apparatus.

anchors.title: A Detailed Examination of Methods for Unifying, Simplifying and Extending Several Results About Self-Justifying Logics
anchors.summary: Defines a generic configuration as a tuple of language, bounded formula class, base axiom system, deduction method, and proof-code encoding.
anchors.next_as_action: Use this tuple as the checklist for any AS proof-apparatus ADR that claims SJAS-level relevance.

anchors.title: A Detailed Examination of Methods for Unifying, Simplifying and Extending Several Results About Self-Justifying Logics
anchors.summary: Defines Level(k) consistency as the nonexistence of proofs of both a Pi_k sentence and its Sigma_k negation.
anchors.next_as_action: Do not use the phrase self-consistency in AS without specifying the level and negation vocabulary.

anchors.title: A Detailed Examination of Methods for Unifying, Simplifying and Extending Several Results About Self-Justifying Logics
anchors.summary: Defines SelfCons_k(beta,d) as a self-referencing Pi_1 sentence declaring level-k consistency of beta plus that sentence.
anchors.next_as_action: Make proof-code substitution and self-reference explicit before extending AS proof certificates beyond manifest examples.

anchors.title: A Detailed Examination of Methods for Unifying, Simplifying and Extending Several Results About Self-Justifying Logics
anchors.summary: States that an EA-stable generic configuration yields a self-justifying system of the form B + SelfCons_1(B,d).
anchors.next_as_action: If AS wants a positive self-justification theorem, identify which stability condition its chosen configuration can satisfy.

anchors.title: On How the Introducing of a New theta Function Symbol Into Arithmetic's Formalism Is Germane to Devising Axiom Systems that Can Appreciate Fragments of Their Own Hilbert Consistency
anchors.summary: Defines Hilbert-style deduction as a modus-ponens apparatus satisfying Godel completeness.
anchors.next_as_action: Keep Hilbert-style proof claims separate from tableau and certificate-checker claims in AS.

anchors.title: On How the Introducing of a New theta Function Symbol Into Arithmetic's Formalism Is Germane to Devising Axiom Systems that Can Appreciate Fragments of Their Own Hilbert Consistency
anchors.summary: Defines a self-justifying configuration as a pair whose theorems include a consistency statement for its deduction method and whose axiom system is in fact consistent.
anchors.next_as_action: Use the pair-of-axiom-basis-and-deduction-method framing when AS states any formal-confidence target.

anchors.title: On How the Introducing of a New theta Function Symbol Into Arithmetic's Formalism Is Germane to Devising Axiom Systems that Can Appreciate Fragments of Their Own Hilbert Consistency
anchors.summary: Distinguishes 1-definitive function symbols from indeterminate ones and introduces theta as the relevant indeterminate operator.
anchors.next_as_action: Do not collapse theta-style indeterminacy into ordinary total function semantics if AS models IQFS.

anchors.title: On How the Introducing of a New theta Function Symbol Into Arithmetic's Formalism Is Germane to Devising Axiom Systems that Can Appreciate Fragments of Their Own Hilbert Consistency
anchors.summary: States that Conjecture 6.6 implies the IQFS framework is consistency preserving under the paper's assumptions.
anchors.next_as_action: Treat IQFS as conjecture-dependent until AS has either accepted, replaced, or independently checked the Conjecture 6.6 step.

anchors.title: How the Law of Excluded Middle Pertains to the Second Incompleteness Theorem and its Boundary-Case Exceptions
anchors.summary: Defines a GenAC pair as self-justifying when it proves consistency of the deduction apparatus over the axiom basis and the basis is actually consistent.
anchors.next_as_action: State future AS proof apparatus claims as pairs of axiom basis and deduction apparatus, not as undifferentiated theorem-prover claims.

anchors.title: How the Law of Excluded Middle Pertains to the Second Incompleteness Theorem and its Boundary-Case Exceptions
anchors.summary: Classifies GenAC systems by which successor, addition, and multiplication totality statements they prove: Type-NS, Type-S, Type-A, and Type-M.
anchors.next_as_action: Tag any AS arithmetic-language proposal with its Type-NS/S/A/M status before discussing self-consistency.

anchors.title: How the Law of Excluded Middle Pertains to the Second Incompleteness Theorem and its Boundary-Case Exceptions
anchors.locus: Section 4, Tab, Xtab, Tab-1, and Level-1 appreciation
anchors.summary: Separates ordinary tableau, tableau with excluded-middle axioms, and Tab-1, then defines Level-1 appreciation of self-consistency for the apparatus.
anchors.next_as_action: When AS revisits tableaux, specify whether excluded middle is theorem-only or an axiom schema because the distinction changes consistency behavior.

anchors.title: How the Law of Excluded Middle Pertains to the Second Incompleteness Theorem and its Boundary-Case Exceptions
anchors.summary: Contrasts consistency-preserving tableau mappings with the failure of the Xtab mapping when excluded middle is promoted to logical axiom status.
anchors.next_as_action: Use this as a hard boundary for future AS proof-apparatus options: proof-search equivalence is not enough when self-reference changes the axiom basis.
